title: Mamba: Linear-Time Sequence Modeling with Selective State Spaces
hook: Mamba lets neural networks process very long sequences, without relying on attention.
promise: You will understand how Mamba models long sequences efficiently using selective state spaces instead of attention.
prerequisite_note: Bring an openness to how sequences are modeled and a curiosity about alternatives to attention.

glossary.plain: A neural architecture that tracks and updates only the most relevant information over time.
An algorithm whose computation grows in direct proportion to the input length.
A method allowing models to focus on important parts of the input when making predictions.
Techniques for handling data where the input consists of many steps or tokens.
The process of changing the internal memory of a model at each step.
Combining information from different positions in a sequence to make predictions.

cards.title: A new way to handle long sequences
body: See how Mamba sidesteps attention to model long sequences quickly and efficiently.

cards.title: Why long sequences are hard
body: Imagine reading a book where you must remember every word to understand the next. For neural networks, handling very long sequences is a huge challenge.
Transformers use attention, but this becomes slow and memory-hungry as the sequence grows. Is there another way to keep track of what matters most?

cards.title: From input to output: Mamba's selective flow
body: Mamba starts by receiving a stream of tokens, just like a Transformer. But instead of attending to every token, it keeps a compact state.
At each step, Mamba selectively updates this state, focusing only on the most relevant parts. The updated state then predicts the next output efficiently.
cards.caption: Mamba compresses sequence history into a state, updating only what matters at each step.

cards.title: Attention vs. Selective State Spaces
body: Toggle to see how modeling long sequences differs between attention and Mamba.
cards.offNote: Each token compares itself to every other, causing slowdowns for long sequences.
cards.onNote: Only the relevant state is updated, so computation stays fast as sequences grow.

cards.title: The selective state space trick
body: Instead of letting every token interact with every other, Mamba keeps a dynamic memory that evolves as new tokens arrive.
This memory, or state, is updated using learned rules that let the model decide what to keep and what to ignore, making it both efficient and expressive.

cards.title: Sequence length vs. computation time
body: Drag to see how Mamba's speed holds up as sequence length increases.
cards.low: Both attention and Mamba are fast when the sequence is short.
cards.mid: Attention slows down, but Mamba stays efficient as the sequence grows.
cards.high: Mamba remains linear, while attention becomes impractically slow and memory-hungry.

cards.title: What makes Mamba run?
body: Pick the essential ingredients for Mamba's selective state space model.

choices.label: Selective State Update
choices.note: Ensures only relevant information is kept at each step.

choices.label: Linear-Time Computation
choices.note: Keeps processing fast, even for long sequences.

choices.label: Dynamic Parameterization
choices.note: Learns how to update the state in context.

choices.label: Input Projection
choices.note: Maps raw tokens into a form the model can use.

choices.label: Output Projection
choices.note: Converts the internal state into predictions.

cards.title: Mamba's impact on sequence modeling
body: Mamba matches or outperforms attention-based models on long-sequence tasks, while using less memory and time.
Its linear scaling makes it practical for tasks previously out of reach for attention-heavy architectures.
cards.caption: Mamba's efficient architecture unlocks long-range modeling that attention struggles with.

cards.title: Selective state spaces join the canon
body: Mamba showed that attention is not the only way to handle long sequences. Selective state spaces are now a real alternative.
By rethinking how models store and update information, Mamba opened the door for efficient, scalable sequence modeling in AI.

recap.label: Receives
recap.note: Take in input tokens as a stream

recap.label: Selects
recap.note: Update only the most relevant internal state

recap.label: Predicts
recap.note: Use the compact state to generate outputs
